name: Patient Portal Access - Ages 12-17
description: Portal request for adolescent patients with privacy-aware consent.

content.title: Young adult full proxy access
content.text: This request gives another authorized person access to the patient medical record through FollowMyHealth.

content.title: Authorization to release protected health information
items: Proxy access lets another authorized person access medical information through FollowMyHealth.
Proxy access for an adult patient is valid until revoked by the patient.
Proxy access for a child account is valid until the child turns 12. After age 12, proxy permission must be authorized by the patient.

Information released through portal access may include sensitive information, including sexually transmitted disease, HIV/AIDS, behavioral health, mental health, and alcohol or drug treatment information.
Authorization is voluntary and may be refused.
Authorization may be revoked in writing, but revocation does not apply to information already released.
Information disclosed through portal access may be re-disclosed and may no longer be protected by federal and state privacy rules.
The authorization must be complete, signed, and dated. Portal proxy activation must occur within 30 days from the authorization date.

content.title: Bright Futures 1 Month Visit
content.text: Please answer all questions so the clinic can understand how things are going and prepare for the visit.

sections.title: What Would You Like To Talk About Today?

fields.label: Concerns, questions, or problems to discuss today
How you are feeling
options: Getting back to work or school

fields.label: Your baby and family
options: Asking for help when you need it
Community services that may be able to help your family

fields.label: Getting to know your baby
options: How to keep your baby safe while sleeping
Tummy time for playtime with you

fields.label: Feeding your baby
options: How often you should feed your baby
How to know your baby is getting enough
How to hold your baby while feeding

fields.label: Safety
options: Choking from bracelets, necklaces, and toys with loops or strings

fields.label: Have any of your baby's relatives developed new medical problems since your last visit?
If yes, describe new medical problems
Do you have concerns about how your child sees?
Has a family member or contact had tuberculosis or a positive tuberculin skin test?
Was your child born in a country at high risk for tuberculosis?
Has your child traveled for longer than 1 week to a country at high risk for tuberculosis?
Does your child have any special health care needs?
If yes, describe special health care needs
Other than your baby's birth, have there been any major changes in your family lately?
Over the past 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by little interest or pleasure in doing things?
Over the past 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by feeling down, depressed, or hopeless?
Does your child live with anyone who uses tobacco or spend time where people smoke?
Do you have specific concerns about your baby's development, learning, or behavior?
If yes, describe development, learning, or behavior concerns
Check off each task that your baby is able to do
1. I have been able to laugh and see the funny side of things
options: As much as I always could

fields.label: 2. I have looked forward with enjoyment to things
options: As much as I ever did
Rather less than I used to
Definitely less than I used to

fields.label: 3. I have blamed myself unnecessarily when things went wrong
4. I have been anxious or worried for no good reason
5. I have felt scared or panicky for no very good reason
6. Things have been getting on top of me
options: Yes, most of the time I haven't been able to cope at all
Yes, sometimes I haven't been coping as well as usual
No, most of the time I have coped quite well
No, I have been coping as well as ever

fields.label: 7. I have been so unhappy that I have had difficulty sleeping
8. I have felt sad or miserable
9. I have been so unhappy that I have been crying
10. The thought of harming myself has occurred to me

content.title: EPDS instructions
content.text: Please choose the answer that comes closest to how you have felt in the past 7 days, not just how you feel today.

fields.label: Within the past 12 months, did you worry your food would run out before you got money to buy more?
Within the past 12 months, did the food you bought not last and you didn't have money to get more?
Do you worry about losing your housing?
Within the past 12 months, have you or family members been unable to get heat, electricity, or water when really needed?
Within the past 12 months, has lack of transportation kept you from appointments, medicines, work, or getting things you need?
Do you feel physically and emotionally safe where you currently live?
Within the past 12 months, have you been hit, slapped, kicked, or otherwise physically hurt by someone?
Within the past 12 months, have you been disgraced or emotionally abused by your partner or ex-partner?
Do you have urgent needs such as no food today, no place to sleep tonight, no ride home or to work, or fear of being hurt if you go home?

fields.label: Does your baby make sounds like da, ga, ka, and ba?
If you copy sounds your baby makes, does your baby repeat the same sounds back to you?

If you ask your baby to, does your baby play at least one nursery game even if you do not show the activity yourself?
Does your baby follow one simple command, such as Come here, Give it to me, or Put it back?
Does your baby say three words, such as Mama, Dada, and Baba?
If you hold both hands just to balance your baby, does your baby support own weight while standing?
When sitting on the floor, does your baby sit up straight for several minutes without using hands for support?
Does your baby crawl, creep, or scoot on hands and knees?
Does your baby pull to standing while holding onto furniture?
Does your baby get into a sitting position without help?
Does your baby walk beside furniture while holding on with only one hand?
Does your baby pick up a small toy with only one hand?
Does your baby successfully pick up a crumb or Cheerio using thumb and all fingers in a raking motion?
Does your baby pick up a small toy with the tips of thumb and fingers?
After one or two tries, does your baby pick up a piece of string with first finger and thumb?
Does your baby pick up a crumb or Cheerio with the tips of thumb and finger?
Does your baby put a small toy down without dropping it and then take the hand off the toy?
Does your baby pass a toy back and forth from one hand to the other?
Does your baby pick up two small toys, one in each hand, and hold them for about one minute?
When holding a toy, does your baby bang it against another toy on the table?
While holding a small toy in each hand, does your baby clap the toys together?
Does your baby poke at or try to get a crumb or Cheerio that is inside a clear bottle?
After watching you hide a small toy under a piece of paper or cloth, does your baby find it?
While lying on the back, does your baby put a foot in the mouth?
Does your baby drink water, juice, or formula from a cup while you hold it?
Does your baby feed self a cracker or cookie?
When you hold out your hand and ask for a toy, does your baby offer it even if not letting go?
When you dress your baby, does your baby push arm through a sleeve once arm is started?
When you hold out your hand and ask for a toy, does your baby let go of it into your hand?
Uses both hands and both legs equally well?

content.title: About this test
content.text: The Asthma Control Test provides a numerical score to help assess asthma control and should be discussed with the doctor.

content.title: Scoring note
content.text: If the total score is 19 or less, asthma may not be under control. Be sure to talk with the doctor about the results.

content.title: Answer scale
content.text: Each of the 5 scored questions is answered from least controlled to most controlled. Scores are intended to be totaled for clinical review.

description: C-ACT screener for children ages 4 through 11.

content.title: How to take the Childhood Asthma Control Test
items: Step 1: Let the child respond to questions 1 through 4. You may help with reading or understanding, but let the child select the response.
Complete questions 5 through 7 on your own without letting the child response influence your answers.
There are no right or wrong answers.
Step 2: Record each answer score.
Step 3: Add the score boxes for the total.
Step 4: Take the test to the doctor to discuss the total score.

fields.label: How much of a problem is asthma when you run, exercise, or play sports?

Do you wake up during the night because of your asthma?

Woke up at night because of asthma in the past 4 weeks

content.title: Scoring note
content.text: If the child score is 19 or less, it may be a sign that asthma is not controlled as well as it could be. Bring the test to the doctor to discuss the results.

name: Wart Treatment Consent
description: Consent for wart treatment.

content.title: Consent for wart treatment
content.text: The patient has been diagnosed with a wart or molluscum contagiosum. Please review the treatment acknowledgements before signing.

fields.label: I understand possible risks, discomfort, blistering, pigment change, scarring, recurrence, or need for repeat treatment.

content.title: Treatment acknowledgements
items: There is no single treatment that can guarantee successful treatment of warts.
Treatment may require one or more methods or combinations of several methods.
Multiple treatments and multiple office visits may be required.
The treated area may develop new lesions, recurrences of previously treated lesions, or scarring.
Signing represents willingness to proceed with the procedure while understanding these statements.

content.title: Cost and insurance acknowledgement
items: In-office treatment is a surgical procedure and is billed accordingly.
Costs not covered by insurance are the responsibility of the guarantor.
An office visit is charged only if the provider is also evaluating or managing care not related to wart treatment.
Questions about cost may be discussed with the business office at 952-512-5625.
Co-payments, unmet deductibles, non-coverage, or other insurance balances are the responsibility of the guarantor.
